contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 30
- Description: Autopilot /chat seamless-experience patches: SSE heartbeat + mid-round interjection (1 PR + prod redeploy)
  
  Pull requests (GitHub evidence):
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/truesight_autopilot/pull/28
  
  Details:
  SSE heartbeat + mid-round queue interjection for /chat. Two surgical patches that deliver "seamless" experience without a full WebSocket migration.
  
  Heartbeat: wraps each blocking LLM call (via asyncio.to_thread) and each tool call with _heartbeat_until_done(), an async generator that yields SSE heartbeat events every 15s while the awaitable is pending. Direct cause of fix: yesterday's deploy_autopilot call ran 40s and the SSE connection died with ChunkedEncodingError because no bytes flowed.
  
  Mid-round interjection: at the top of each tool-call round, drain queued messages into chat history before the next LLM call. Lifts interjection visibility from "next turn" to "next round of current turn" — POST /chat/queue mid-stream lands on the very next round.
  
  Verified end-to-end locally: heartbeat fired at 15s during LLM wait; interjection POSTed 6s in was queued, picked up between rounds, LLM replied with the requested literal token in a subsequent round.
  
  Shipped:
  - truesight_autopilot#28 (chat: SSE heartbeat + mid-round interjection)
  - Deployed to prod EC2: now at 13fd8ac, systemd active
  
  Why these patches instead of WebSockets: ~10x less engineering for the same user-visible benefit at solo-operator scale. WebSockets remain available later if/when concurrent operators or true bidirectional needs emerge.
- Contributor(s): Gary Teh
- TDG Issued: 50.00
- Attached Filename: N/A
- Destination Contribution File Location: N/A
